# Getting Started

## Install and launching R Packages

The code chunk below uses p_load() of pacman to check if tidyverse packages are installed in the computer. If ther are, then they will be launched into R.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
pacman::p_load(tidyverse)
```
:::


## Importing the data


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
exam_data <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")
```
:::


# Plotting a simple bar chart

::: panel-tabset
## The plot


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex01_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){width=672}
:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
geom_bar: na.rm = FALSE, just = 0.5, lineend = butt, linejoin = mitre
stat_count: na.rm = FALSE
position_stack 
```


:::
:::


## The code


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(data = exam_data, aes(x=RACE))
  geom_bar()
```
:::

:::
